subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Hypothermia Stages
description: Learn Hypothermia Stages in one minute.

hook.text: Hypothermia begins at just 95°F—only 3.6 degrees below normal. That small drop changes everything.

buildup.text: Mild hypothermia (90-95°F): shivering, confusion, clumsiness. The brain starts to slow.

discovery.text: Moderate hypothermia (82-90°F): shivering stops, speech slurs, muscles stiffen, drowsiness takes over.

twist.text: Severe hypothermia (below 82°F): the heart rhythm becomes erratic. Victims often appear dead.

climax.text: The medical rule: 'No one is dead until they are warm and dead'—cold brains can survive.

punchline.text: 3.6 degrees starts it. 16 can kill. Not dead until warm and dead.

quiz.question: At what core temperature does hypothermia begin?
